highlights.text: It is for this reason I was looking for higher Monday and Tuesday, stating on Friday at 4pm: “My lean as long as those hold we work higher to 6806, 6815, then 6851.” We hit 6851+ Monday.
Then I again stated Monday at 4pm: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
As I’ve discussed at length, this is because ES cycles between two types of price action which I call the two siblings: These siblings are elevator down , and short squeeze.
Case in point: Friday morning ES went elevator down selling 115 points.
When you see an elevator down move like this, we know an equivalently (at minimum) powerful short squeeze is loading.
The same thing: A Failed Breakdown (my core setup) where price flushes a major low, traps shorts, and recovers.
I wrote Thursday at 4pm: “It is much smarter to wait for the Failed Breakdown of the October 22nd 6690 low.” We got this at 1:21PM Friday and we began what ended up being a 220+ point leg higher.
This Failed Breakdown did not occur in technical isolation though.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the Friday afternoon Failed Breakdown that caused this rally.
What made it a macro Failed Breakdown and how to trade it?

post_title: After 3 Full Days of Squeeze, SPX Takes A Breather Today. Will It Just Setup More Upside? November 13 Plan
